mechanism_narrative: Sustained decline in the federal funds rate over a 3-month window signals monetary easing that reduces the discount rate applied to equity valuations, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns. The mechanism operates continuously without threshold activation.
evidence_refs: fact:current FEDFUNDS at 3.04% provides room for easing
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.03 over any 2-year rolling window, or 20-day forward returns following FEDFUNDS declines are not significantly higher than baseline (t-test p > 0.05).

mechanism_narrative: Unemployment rate rises above 5.5% within a 2-month window, signaling labor market deterioration that triggers equity risk-off positioning and negative S&P 500 forward returns as investors anticipate earnings compression and recession risk.
evidence_refs: fact:current UNRATE at 5.0% is near trigger threshold
regime_hint: late-cycle expansion with labor market softening
failure_condition: SPX produces positive 20-day returns in more than 55% of instances where UNRATE exceeds 5.5%, or Rank IC for UNRATE as predictor of negative returns falls below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Retail sales (RSAFS) and housing starts (HOUST) move in opposite directions over a 2-month period, with RSAFS declining while HOUST remains flat or rises. This divergence signals consumer demand weakness despite housing resilience, indicating demand-side fragmentation that produces negative equity returns as growth expectations compress.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS declines 2% month-over-month while HOUST remains within 1% of prior month
evidence_refs: fact:current RSAFS at 350775M, HOUST at 2009K units
failure_condition: Interaction term (RSAFS decline × HOUST stability) produces Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX 60-day returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) declines more than 0.5% month-over-month, signaling supply-side contraction that constrains earnings growth and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets immediately reprice growth expectations downward.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns following INDPRO declines > 0.5% are positive in more than 52% of instances, or Rank IC for INDPRO as negative return predictor falls below 0.02 over any 2-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Payroll employment (PAYEMS) growth decelerates to below 50,000 net new jobs per month over a 3-month rolling average, signaling labor market momentum loss that precedes earnings downgrades and produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: PAYEMS 3-month rolling average growth falls below 50,000 jobs per month
failure_condition: SPX 20-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where PAYEMS growth falls below 50K/month, or Rank IC for PAYEMS deceleration as negative return predictor falls below 0.03 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Inflation (CPIAUCSL) accelerates above 3.5% year-over-year while the federal funds rate remains below 4.0%, creating a negative real rate environment that signals monetary policy lag. This regime produces positive S&P 500 returns as real asset values rise and equity serves as inflation hedge.
candidate_trigger: CPIAUCSL year-over-year growth exceeds 3.5% AND FEDFUNDS remains below 4.0%
regime_hint: accommodative monetary policy with elevated inflation
failure_condition: Interaction term (high inflation × low rates) produces Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX 60-day returns are negative in more than 48% of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Real GDP growth (GDPC1) decelerates to below 1.5% annualized rate in the most recent quarter, signaling demand weakness that produces negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days as earnings growth expectations compress and recession risk increases.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 annualized growth rate falls below 1.5%
failure_condition: SPX 60-day forward returns are positive in more than 55% of instances where GDPC1 growth falls below 1.5%, or Rank IC for GDPC1 deceleration as negative return predictor falls below 0.05 over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Industrial production (INDPRO) growth accelerates above 0.8% month-over-month while payroll employment (PAYEMS) simultaneously grows above 150,000 jobs per month, signaling synchronized supply and labor expansion that produces positive S&P 500 forward returns over 20 trading days as earnings growth accelerates.
candidate_trigger: INDPRO monthly growth exceeds 0.8% AND PAYEMS monthly growth exceeds 150,000 jobs
failure_condition: Interaction term (strong INDPRO × strong PAYEMS) produces Rank IC below 0.04 over any 2-year rolling window, or SPX 20-day returns are negative in more than 48% of triggered instances.
